ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic sandwich class; no specific entry for chilli tuna and egg sandwich set exists. Mapped to closest parent class FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers sandwiches and filled rolls as a category. No specific chilli tuna egg sandwich set code exists; this is the closest defensible parent node.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for this composite set meal. SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not extend to this level of specificity for mixed set meals.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal comprising a chilli tuna and egg sandwich made with two slices of toasted white bread, served with a brewed beverage (coffee or tea) and a slice of sponge or butter cake, totalling approximately 550 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy: canned tuna in water (FDC ID 175159), hard-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424), white bread (FDC ID 353340), mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), chilli sauce (FDC ID 170162), sponge cake (FDC ID 18139)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA SR Legacy or HPB Singapore Foodo entry exists for a chilli tuna and egg sandwich set as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging across USDA SR Legacy entries for canned tuna in water (FDC ID 175159), hard-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424), white bread (FDC ID 353340), mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), chilli sauce (FDC ID 170162), and sponge cake (FDC ID 18139), proportioned to reflect a typical Singaporean kopitiam sandwich set. Input values from the source record (confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against these USDA SR Legacy component references and retained where consistent. Singapore HPB Foodo database was also consulted for regional portion norms. USDA SR Legacy is an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for a chilli tuna and egg sandwich set. GI estimated at 65 based on the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: white bread (GI 70–75 per University of Sydney GI Database) with documented moderating effect of co-ingested protein (tuna, egg) and fat (mayonnaise), which lower the composite meal GI per Brand-Miller et al. mixed-meal GI methodology published in Diabetes Care (2008). A composite GI of 65 (medium category) is a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 77.0) / 100 = 50.1, where carbohydrate per 550 g serving = 14.0 g/100 g × 5.5 = 77.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 375 mg per 100 g; cumulative sodium from canned tuna, mayonnaise, chilli sauce, white bread, and cake across a 550 g set meal delivers approximately 2,063 mg sodium — approaching or exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced mayonnaise and chilli sauce, choose canned tuna in brine (rinsed) over oil-packed, substitute wholemeal bread for white bread, and avoid sweetened beverages in the set to meaningfully reduce the total sodium and cardiovascular load of this meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: White bread is a high-GI refined carbohydrate (GI ~70–75); the set meal delivers approximately 77 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of ~50, and dietary fibre is low at 1.0 g per 100 g, providing minimal glycaemic buffering despite the protein content from tuna and egg.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should substitute white bread with wholemeal or multigrain bread to lower the GI, omit the sponge or butter cake component entirely, request unsweetened coffee or tea, and retain the tuna and egg filling as a beneficial protein source to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat is moderate at 3.0 g per 100 g and trans fat is present at 0.1 g per 100 g; however, canned tuna provides cardioprotective omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA), partially offsetting the atherogenic risk from mayonnaise and cake components.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this set meal to occasional consumption, choose tuna in brine over oil, reduce mayonnaise quantity, avoid the cake component to lower saturated fat and refined sugar intake, and request unsweetened beverages to reduce overall cardiovascular risk from this meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Canned tuna provides anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA); however, mayonnaise contributes omega-6-rich vegetable oils, white bread contributes to glycaemic load and low-grade inflammation, and chilli contains solanine-related compounds that may aggravate RA in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the tuna component but should substitute wholemeal bread, reduce mayonnaise, avoid the cake component, and monitor individual tolerance to chilli and nightshade-derived ingredients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chilli Tuna and Egg Sandwich Set is a popular Singaporean kopitiam-style set meal consisting of a toasted white bread sandwich filled with canned chilli tuna and egg (hard-boiled or scrambled), served with a brewed beverage (typically kopi or teh — local coffee or tea) and a slice of sponge or butter cake. The sandwich filling combines the savoury, mildly spicy flavour of chilli-seasoned canned tuna with the creamy richness of egg and mayonnaise, sandwiched between two slices of crisp, lightly toasted white bread. The overall flavour profile is savoury-spicy with a soft, moist interior and a slightly crunchy crust. Nutritionally, the set meal provides approximately 225 kcal per 100 g, with 12.5 g protein, 14.0 g fat, and 14.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. The full 550 g set delivers approximately 1,238 kcal, 68.8 g protein, 77.0 g fat, and 77.0 g carbohydrate. Sodium is elevated at 375 mg per 100 g (~2,063 mg per set), making this meal a concern for hypertensive individuals. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), with a glycemic load of approximately 50 per set, which is high for patients with type 2 diabetes. Canned tuna contributes omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) with cardioprotective and anti-inflammatory properties. The meal is a fixture of Singapore's hawker and kopitiam breakfast and lunch culture, reflecting the city-state's multicultural food heritage blending Western sandwich formats with local toast-culture traditions.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component averaging from USDA SR Legacy approved sources (FDC IDs 175159, 173424, 353340, 172741, 170162, 18139), cross-checked against the source record input data (confidence 0.7). No single approved source entry exists for this exact composite set meal. GI is an analogous estimate based on white bread GI from the University of Sydney GI Database with documented mixed-meal moderation methodology per Brand-Miller et al. (Diabetes Care, 2008). Confidence is medium because the values are internally consistent and source-grounded but not derived from a direct measurement of this specific dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite sandwich set is not available as a single validated entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024; component-level USDA data exists but reliable proportioning for the full set meal is insufficient to report these values with confidence. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite set meal is not available as a validated single-entry figure in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or HPB Foodo; not confirmed by an approved source to the required precision. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite set meal are not available as a validated composite entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo as of 2024. Two portions are noted in the input: 200 g (sandwich only) and 550 g (full set); the 550 g full set is used as the default serving for glycemic load calculation. The is_halal flag is set to false as halal certification status of all components (particularly the cake and mayonnaise) cannot be confirmed from available data; consumers should verify with the specific vendor. Chilli component may contain nightshade-derived solanine compounds relevant for rheumatoid arthritis-sensitive individuals. Aliases consistency self-check performed: all 13 string values in aliases_by_language (english: 7, chinese: 2, malay: 1, tamil: 1, hindi: 1, romanized: 1) appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
